Patient Handout

Neurology

BPPV — Treating Vertigo at Home

Step-by-step instructions for the modified Epley procedure to treat Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (BPPV).

BPPV is the most common cause of vertigo — a brief spinning sensation triggered by head movement. The Epley maneuver repositions tiny calcium crystals in your inner ear and relieves symptoms in most people within a few sessions.

Which Ear Is Affected?

The instructions below are for left ear BPPV. If your right ear is affected, perform the same procedure in the opposite direction — starting with your head turned to the right. Not sure which ear? Your doctor can confirm this with a positional test.

The Modified Epley Procedure

Follow these steps in order — hold each position for 30 seconds:

  1. 1
    Start sitting on a bed or firm surface with your head turned 45° to the left. Place a pillow behind you so that when you lie back, it will sit under your shoulders.
  2. 2
    Lie back quickly with your shoulders on the pillow, neck extended, and head resting on the surface. Your left (affected) ear is now pointing downward. Hold for 30 seconds.
  3. 3
    Turn your head 90° to the right (without raising it), so you are now looking up and to the right. Hold for 30 seconds.
  4. 4
    Roll your body and head another 90° to the right (onto your right side, face toward the floor). Hold for 30 seconds.
  5. 5
    Sit up on the right side.

How often to do this:

3 times per session
Repetitions
or
Once daily
Frequency
or
Until free of positional vertigo for 24 hours
Stop when
